what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that registers an image attachment in the design-source channel, pointing to the image asset at image://design-source/capital-raise/p-08 and recording when it was added.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, with no body content
Attachment id att-d9dfd36c matches the filename stem
standouts: The target URI points to a specific page/asset slug p-08 under capital-raise
It is typed as an images attachment
Filename is derived from the attachment id
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a fixed set of metadata fields: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added
Body is left empty after the frontmatter delimiter
concerns: The p-08 slug and capital-raise path are opaque without additional context
No caption, alt text, or asset dimensions are recorded
No author or provenance beyond a timestamp is present
notes: A timestamped image attachment pointer for design-source/capital-raise/p-08 with minimal metadata and no body.
questions: What visual content does p-08 represent?
Who added this attachment and why?
Does the referenced image asset actually exist?
How does this attachment relate to other capital-raise attachments?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is an attachment metadata record and it functions as one: it records an image attachment targeting design-source/capital-raise/p-08.
intention_signals: It appears to register an image asset in the design-source channel so that capital-raise page p-08 can reference or display it.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped, typed pointer from a stable attachment id to an image asset location, enabling media discovery and association without embedding binary data.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment record linking attachment id att-d9dfd36c in the design-source channel to the image asset image://design-source/capital-raise/p-08, recorded on 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to catalog an image attachment for the design-source channel so it could be associated with capital-raise material page p-08.
